goal: Add a side-effect-free runtime dry-run adapter contract that converts a BPK-008 runtime integration contract into a guarded dry-run invocation plan. Do not add a server route, orchestrator call, provider call, database write, GitHub write, package change, deployment change, or frontend file.
scope_out: Do not add executable runtime wiring, server routes, orchestrator calls, provider calls, DB writes, GitHub writes, frontend files, deployment notes, package scripts, dependencies, or environment variables.
claims: Dry-run invocation readiness can be derived from the runtime integration contract without executing runtime behavior.
A dry-run plan must keep write, deploy, provider, DB, and GitHub side effects disabled.
Blocked or review-required integration contracts cannot become dry-run runnable.
The adapter contract is side-effect-free and tested independently.
assumptions: BPK-008 is committed and available as runtime integration contract source.
Actual route/orchestrator integration belongs to a later runtime block.
Dry-run input text is caller-supplied and not interpreted as permission to execute.
dod: builder/src/runtimeDryRunAdapterContract.ts exports side-effect-free dry-run adapter types and planner.
The planner returns ready, review_required, or blocked with deterministic guards.
The planner sets dryRun true and all write/deploy/provider/DB/GitHub side effects false.
builder/tests/runtimeDryRunAdapterContract.test.ts covers ready plan, blocked integration, review integration, missing instruction, write mode blocked, and no-mutation behavior.
review-packets/BPK-013.md records scope, safety invariants, gates, and verification.
The focused runtime dry-run adapter test and npm run typecheck in builder/ pass.
stop_conditions: A server route, orchestrator call, provider call, DB write, GitHub write, deployment change, package change, or frontend file becomes necessary.
prior_task_findings: BPK-008 classified runtime adoption readiness. BPK-013 should create a dry-run adapter contract without wiring it into runtime.
